entry.title: How Israel targets the small bodies of Palestinian kids, with Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant
entry.summary:
In Gaza, Israel killed 13 Palestinians and wounded at least 18 others over a 24-hour period on Tuesday and Wednesday, in a series of drone strikes, shelling and gunfire.
In the pre-dawn hours on Wednesday, Israeli helicopter gunships targeted a home in Deir al-Balah, killing three members of the Abu Qassem family: Omar, 33, his wife Asmaa, 32, and their 6-year-old daughter Habiba.
The killing of children has been a key feature of the ongoing genocide, and the evidence shows it is systematic.
Recently, an independent United Nations Commission of Inquiry concluded that Israel’s deliberate targeting of children in Gaza establishes, in part, its genocidal intent to destroy Palestinian society.
We are joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. They are co-authors of a new report, “Lethal Precision Without Accountability: Israeli ‘Quadcopter’ Use in Gaza,” which documents Israel’s patterns of targeting children.
Dr. Syed is an emergency medicine physician who worked in Gaza during Israel’s genocide. Bryant is a Pentagon whistleblower and a war crimes analyst, and served as a special advisor to the United Nations International Commission of Inquiry on Palestine.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1014 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fwS9YbgaPUg
Theme Music by Greg Wilson
Your gift supports The Electronic Intifada's independent journalism on Palestine: • Donate by credit card, ApplePay, PayPal, Venmo or US bank account via Kindful: https://bit.ly/EIKindful

entry.content:
In Gaza, Israel killed 13 Palestinians and wounded at least 18 others over a 24-hour period on Tuesday and Wednesday, in a series of drone strikes, shelling and gunfire.

In the pre-dawn hours on Wednesday, Israeli helicopter gunships targeted a home in Deir al-Balah, killing three members of the Abu Qassem family: Omar, 33, his wife Asmaa, 32, and their 6-year-old daughter Habiba.

The killing of children has been a key feature of the ongoing genocide, and the evidence shows it is systematic.

Recently, an independent United Nations Commission of Inquiry concluded that Israel’s deliberate targeting of children in Gaza establishes, in part, its genocidal intent to destroy Palestinian society.

We are joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. They are co-authors of a new report, “Lethal Precision Without Accountability: Israeli ‘Quadcopter’ Use in Gaza,” which documents Israel’s patterns of targeting children.

Dr. Syed is an emergency medicine physician who worked in Gaza during Israel’s genocide. Bryant is a Pentagon whistleblower and a war crimes analyst, and served as a special advisor to the United Nations International Commission of Inquiry on Palestine.

This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1014 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fwS9YbgaPUg

entry.title: CIA’s Secret Armies: The Truth About Proxy Warfare
entry.summary:
Does the CIA use secret armies to shape global politics? In this documentary, Syriana Analysis explores the hidden history of proxy warfare and the controversial strategies used to influence conflicts from Afghanistan to the modern-day crisis in Ukraine. We examine the evolution of "contract armies" and the long-term consequences of covert operations.
In this video, we uncover:
- The Origins of Proxy Warfare: How the 1970s conflict in Afghanistan set the stage for decades of covert intervention. - Operation Timber Sycamore: An in-depth look at one of the costliest operations in history and its impact on the Syrian conflict. - The "Contract Army" Theory: Analyzing expert insights from Professor Michael Hudson and Scott Ritter on the use of non-conventional forces. - Geopolitical Consequences: Why these strategies are often shrouded in secrecy and what they mean for the future of international relations.
Featuring archival footage and expert testimony, we piece together a narrative that challenges the official story of global "terror" and examines the geopolitical tools used by major powers.
Timestamps: 0:00 – The Official Story vs. The Hidden History 1:05 – Afghanistan: The Birth of the Proxy Model 3:20 – Hillary Clinton’s Confessions on Funding Groups 5:15 – Are Certain Groups "Contract Armies"? 7:30 – Timber Sycamore: The War for Syria 10:00 – Scott Ritter on the Criminality of Secret Aid 12:15 – Jeffrey Sachs: The Destruction of Syria 15:35 – The Ukraine Connection: A New Front? 18:00 – Final Analysis: Are These Geopolitical Tools?
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
⚠️ This documentary is presented for educational, journalistic, and historical analysis. It examines publicly available historical sources, official statements, and archival material. The inclusion of historical footage or quotations does not constitute endorsement of any organization or individual discussed.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#CIA #ProxyWar #Documentary #SyrianaAnalysis #ForeignPolicy #Geopolitics #TimberSycamore #HistoryExposed

entry.title: Trump restarts war on Iran, but has no way out: The US lost
entry.summary:
Donald Trump violated the diplomatic agreement and restarted the war, bombing Iran and re-imposing a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Ben Norton explains why the US government will fail, because it has no plan and lacks leverage, whereas Tehran and its decentralized military have escalation dominance.

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #77: Why the Iran War Is Far From Over | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
Is the U.S. sleepwalking into a permanent conflict with Iran, or is there a deeper strategic "siege" at play? In episode #77 of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian and historian Tarik Cyril Amar break down the escalating tensions in the Persian Gulf, the leverage of the Strait of Hormuz, and why diplomacy like the JCPOA and recent MOUs may have been destined to fail.
We also dive into Trump’s latest claims of election interference, the role of intelligence services in foreign elections, and a critical look back at Angela Merkel’s legacy—could the war in Ukraine have been avoided with different leadership in 2008?
Join us live every Friday for Cold War 2.0, where we examine the week's biggest geopolitical developments with historical context and strategic analysis.
📅 Every Friday 🕚 11:00 ET | 17:00 CET
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
00:00 – Intro & Channel Update: New Weekly Schedule 01:21 – Analyzing Mainstream Media: The FT on Iran & the U.S. 06:50 – Iran’s Leverage: Control of the Strait of Hormuz 13:14 – Counterarguments: Is the U.S. Still in Command? 21:03 – Historical Analogies: Lessons from Napoleon and 1941 30:21 – Deterrence Strategies: Should Iran Pursue Nuclear Weapons? 40:12 – Target Selection: Why Iran Focuses on Bahrain and Kuwait 48:11 – Globalist Agendas & European Elite Capture 50:07 – Trump’s Claims: Election Interference & Chinese Influence 53:13 – The Role of Intelligence Services in Foreign Elections 59:34 – Ukraine & Merkel’s Legacy: Could the War Have Been Avoided? 01:08:26 – NATO's 2008 Bucharest Summit & Its Consequences 01:11:01 – Final Thoughts & Upcoming Weekly Content
📢 Disclaimer: Guest opinions featured in this video do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Geopolitics #ColdWar2 #Iran #USForeignPolicy #TarikCyrilAmar #SyrianaAnalysis

entry.title: 'Strategic entrapment': Dennis Kucinich on disastrous US-Israeli military merger
entry.summary:
Former US Rep. Dennis Kucinich joins The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal to discuss Section 219, a measure insidiously inserted into the NDAA bill which will effectively merge the US and Israeli militaries across areas including AI, cyberwarfare, biotech, missile defense, and defense industrial production.
Kucinch explains why the bill will shatter what's left of American sovereignty and congressional oversight, contaminating the US military with Israeli spy technology, encouraging new levels of domestic repression while essentially forbidding any disentanglement from the strategically calamitous special relationship with Israel.

entry.title: Trump's Massive Strike Plan for Iran | Elijah J. Magnier
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive analysis, renowned journalist and war correspondent Elijah J. Magnier joins us to unpack the reported U.S. plans for "devastating" strikes against Iran. We explore the strategic objectives behind these maneuvers, the critical role of global oil reserves, and the potential consequences of targeting Iranian infrastructure.
Magnier provides expert insight into Iran’s defensive capabilities, its leverage in the Strait of Hormuz, and why a ground invasion remains a geographical and military impossibility. We also discuss the shifting dynamics in Iraq, the legacy of the Soleimani assassination, and how regional players like the UAE are navigating the tension through strategic acquisitions like the Russian S-400 systems.
Follow Elijah J. Magnier: https://x.com/ejmalrai Read Elijah J. Magnier's Work: https://ejmagnier.com/

entry.title: LIVE From Iran: The Truth About Iran's New Supreme Leader | Ehsan Safarnejad
entry.summary:
The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has transformed Iran's political landscape—but who is his successor, and what is really happening inside the country?
Joining me LIVE from Iran, political analyst Ehsan Safarnejad shares his firsthand experience after attending the funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei and explains the realities behind Iran's new Supreme Leader, the public mood, and the country's evolving political system.
We also examine the renewed military confrontation between the United States and Iran, why the latest diplomatic efforts appear to be collapsing, and whether the region is entering a prolonged period of escalation.
Follow Ehsan Safarnejad on X: https://x.com/Safarnejad_IR/ and check out his channel on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn-_UlidwJz0jTP1opyZbmw

entry.title: 'Millions of enraged people' hunting for revenge -top Iranian analyst on war's terrifying next phase
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal meets top Iranian analyst and journalist Seyed Mostafa Khoshcheshm in Tehran on July 9, moments after the US resumed airstrikes on Iran, and as the funeral procession for the assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei proceeded through Iraq.
Blumenthal asks Khoshcheshm what the red flags of revenge seen throughout the funeral procession signify, and whether the many public calls for targeting the US president and his inner circle as retaliation for Khamenei's assassination, and the killings of other top Iranian leaders, are to be taken seriously.
Khoshcheshm explains why he sees a massive regional war over the horizon, and why this war may eventually reach the United States due to Trump's reckless behavior and "betrayal" of his own regional allies.
Seyed Mostafa Khoshcheshm has taught at a number of Iranian universities and academic centers, including the International Relations Faculty of the Iranian Foreign Ministry.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com

entry.title: 30 Minutes of Lindsey Graham Pushing for War
entry.summary:
Who is the real Lindsey Graham?
For decades, Senator Lindsey Graham has been one of Washington's most outspoken advocates of military intervention. From Iraq and Libya to Syria, Gaza, Iran and Ukraine, he has consistently argued for military escalation while dismissing the human cost of war.
In this episode, Kevork Almassian revisits Lindsey Graham's own words and public statements to examine his political legacy, his foreign policy record, and the consequences of America's interventionist doctrine.
Topics covered: - Lindsey Graham's calls for war across multiple conflicts. - His rhetoric on Iran, Gaza, Syria, Russia and Ukraine. - The human cost of interventionist foreign policy. - Has Washington learned anything from decades of war? - What does Lindsey Graham's record tell us about the U.S. foreign policy establishment?
This video presents publicly available statements for discussion and analysis. Viewers are encouraged to evaluate the evidence and draw their own conclusions.

entry.title: Top Iranian official to The Grayzone: 'The people will not let go of the blood of their Imam'
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal interviews Tehran Mayor Alireza Zakani after he presided over the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei – the largest gathering in human history, where millions clamored for revenge for their leader's assassination.
Zakani is a leading conservative politician who campaigned for the presidency as an opponent of the JCPOA nuclear deal, and has since emerged as top critic of the MOU between Iran and the US.
In this exclusive interview with The Grayzone, Zakani addressed the popular calls for vengeance against Trump and Netanyahu during Khamenei's funeral, and whether they expressed an official policy.
As a former friend and ally of Maj. Gen. Qassem Soleimani and former President Ebrahim Raisi, Zakani discussed the impact of their deaths on the Iranian nation, and addressed whether Iran responded strongly enough to Soleimani's assassination.
Zakani explained his critical views on the MOU, and provided his perspective on the deadly riots that rocked Iran this January as well as the devastating US-Israeli military campaign that followed.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com
Sign up for our Substack: https://substack.com/@thegrayzone
Support our original journalism at Patreon: https://patreon.com/grayzone
Facebook: https://facebook.com/thegrayzone Twitter: https://twitter.com/thegrayzonenews Instagram: https://instagram.com/thegrayzonenews
#TheGrayzone

entry.title: The US is NOT a wealthy country: This is the real data
entry.summary:
Many Western economists and media outlets claim that Americans are wealthier than Europeans, but this is not true; it's based on a misunderstanding of the difference between average wealth and median wealth. Ben Norton explains how the extreme inequality in the US make its average wealth misleadingly look high, and why GDP per capita is not a good measurement. He debunks the myth of "Europoors", which is often spread by Donald Trump supporters.
Topics 0:00 The myth that Americans are wealthy 1:15 Average wealth vs median wealth 3:50 Data on US median wealth 6:42 Wealth inequality (Gini coefficient) 9:31 Poverty and homelessness 10:19 USA vs Europe 12:02 Wealth concentration of US elites 13:28 Distribution of growth in income 15:01 GDP per capita is misleading 21:18 US GDP by sector 23:13 Imputed rent and GDP 25:05 Life expectancy, mortality, etc. 27:10 US healthcare system 28:40 US health system vs OECD 32:58 Ridiculous "Europoor" myth 33:32 Trump cuts taxes on rich 34:27 Trump family corruption 36:20 Insider trading 36:56 Myth of American dream 38:18 Outro

entry.title: Israel kills brothers, World Cup party host in Gaza, with Nora Barrows-Friedman

entry.title: Has press freedom watchdog CPJ betrayed Gaza's slain journalists? with Ali Abunimah

entry.title: Iran forces US to face new reality in West Asia, with Helena Cobban
entry.summary:
Overnight, Iran launched attacks on US military targets in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. This came in retaliation for US airstrikes on dozens of sites in Iran.
In recent days, the United States has been escalating threats and violence in West Asia – in flagrant violation of the June ceasefire agreement – while presenting itself as the guardian of stability.
But the basic reality remains the same since Washington and Tel Aviv launched their failed war of aggression and regime change in February: Iran controls the vital Strait of Hormuz and cannot be dislodged by military force.
The escalation in violence comes as millions of people across Iran and Iraq have been joining the funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s leader murdered on the first day of the US-Israeli attack.
To discuss the latest regional developments, including Lebanon and Palestine, we’re joined by Helena Cobban.
She is an analyst with decades of experience covering the region. She directs Just World Educational and writes the newsletter Globalities. She is co-author with Rami Khouri of Understanding Hamas – And Why That Matters.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1007 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by veteran journalist and analyst Helena Cobban. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wfkVIM6gVxw&sttick=0
Theme Music by Greg Wilson

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #76 | Is Israel Planning a False Flag Against Trump? | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian and historian Tarik Cyril Amar dive deep into the latest geopolitical escalations. We begin by analyzing a viral clip of Professor Mohammad Marandi dismantling mainstream media narratives, before shifting to the Wall Street Journal’s explosive report on alleged Iranian "kill lists" targeting Donald Trump. Is this a genuine threat, or a calculated piece of disinformation designed to force a regional war?
We also examine the internal decay of the West, from Germany’s crumbling infrastructure to the European Union’s controversial new "Chat Control" legislation—a massive expansion of surveillance that threatens the privacy of every citizen.
Subscribe to Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.

entry.title: NATO’s Shadow Bank: How the West Plans to Finance the Next Great War | Mats Nilsson
entry.summary:
In this special broadcast, Kevork Almassian is joined by Mats Nilsson, senior analyst at the Dissident Club in Sweden, to peel back the layers of the recent NATO Ankara Summit. While mainstream headlines focus on diplomatic posturing, Nilsson reveals the emergence of a "transatlantic cash machine" designed to bankroll long-term militarization.
We dive deep into the creation of the "Defense Security and Resilience Bank," a multilateral entity that Nilsson argues is a nefarious tool to keep the West on a permanent war footing. We also discuss the shifting roles of regional players like Turkey and Israel, and why the ultimate target of these escalations may lie much further east than Ukraine or Iran.
Follow Mats Nilsson: https://x.com/mazzenilsson Follow Dissident Club: https://dissidentpodden.substack.com/
Topics discussed: - NATO's Ankara Summit: What's the alliance's new strategic direction? - Trump's meeting with Syria's new leadership and the future of the Golan Heights. - Will Turkey finally receive the F-35 despite Israeli objections? - Has the U.S.-Iran conflict entered a new and more dangerous phase? - Europe's military transformation after Ukraine. - What comes next for the Middle East?
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join

entry.title: MI6’s Next Move: Is Venezuela the New Syria? | Kit Klarenberg
entry.summary:
Is the "Syrian Model" of regime change being exported to South America? Investigative journalist Kit Klarenberg joins us to discuss his latest findings on the arrival of the White Helmets in Venezuela following the recent earthquakes.
We dive deep into the history of the White Helmets—from their creation by British intelligence veterans to their role in alternative governance and propaganda in Syria. Is their deployment to Venezuela a humanitarian mission, or a "beachhead" for Western intelligence to penetrate Venezuelan state structures during a moment of extreme vulnerability?
We also explore the disturbing connection between Ukrainian military intelligence and al-Qaeda-linked groups in Africa’s Sahel region, and how these parallel strategies are redrawing the map of global proxy warfare.
Read Kit Klarenberg's investigations: https://substack.com/@kitklarenberg https://www.kitklarenberg.com/ Follow Kit on X: https://x.com/KitKlarenberg

entry.content:
Is the "Syrian Model" of regime change being exported to South America? Investigative journalist Kit Klarenberg joins us to discuss his latest findings on the arrival of the White Helmets in Venezuela following the recent earthquakes.

We dive deep into the history of the White Helmets—from their creation by British intelligence veterans to their role in alternative governance and propaganda in Syria. Is their deployment to Venezuela a humanitarian mission, or a "beachhead" for Western intelligence to penetrate Venezuelan state structures during a moment of extreme vulnerability?

We also explore the disturbing connection between Ukrainian military intelligence and al-Qaeda-linked groups in Africa’s Sahel region, and how these parallel strategies are redrawing the map of global proxy warfare.

entry.title: BREAKING - Macron Arrives in Syria... Then Damascus Explodes
entry.summary:
French President Emmanuel Macron's landmark visit to Syria was overshadowed by explosions in Damascus, raising immediate questions about who was behind the attacks—and who stands to benefit from the timing.
In today's livestream, Kevork Almassian examines France's strategic objectives in Syria, the significance of the Damascus blasts, Netanyahu's controversial claims that Lebanese Christian villages sought annexation by Israel, reports that Donald Trump may approve Turkey's purchase of F-35 fighter jets despite Israeli objections, and the rapidly evolving balance of power in the Levant.
Topics discussed: - Macron's military and economic objectives in Syria - Who benefits from the explosions in Damascus? - Did the attacks send a political message? - Netanyahu's annexation claims and Lebanon's response - Trump, Turkey, and the reported F-35 deal - The new geopolitical contest over Syria and the Eastern Mediterranean

entry.title: Going to Khamenei's funeral with Prof. Marandi
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal joins Prof. Mohammad Marandi on his trip to Tehran's Mosalla to pay respects to Sayyed Ali Khameini.
The public mourning which began on July 4th for the martyred Iranian leader comprises the largest funeral in human history.
Marandi analyzes the significance of Khameini's life and legacy, and discusses his role in forging the Islamic Republic.

entry.title: Iran After Khamenei: The Question Nobody Can Answer | Radical Thinker
entry.summary:
In this special stream, Kevork Almassian is joined by Andy from Radical Thinker to dive deep into the mysteries surrounding Iran’s leadership following the reported assassination of Ali Khamenei. While Mojtaba Khamenei has been named the successor, his absence from his father’s funeral has sparked intense global speculation. Is he injured, in hiding for security, or is something even more complex happening behind the scenes?
In this episode, we discuss:
- The Mojtaba Mystery: Why the new Supreme Leader stayed hidden during the most defiant moment for the Iranian nation. - The "Revenge" Doctrine: Analyzing the calls for justice from key Iranian military and political figures, and what a direct response against Western or Israeli assets could actually look like. - Russia’s Final Warning: The symbolism of Dmitry Medvedev’s appearance at the funeral and his stark comparisons between the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear weapons. - EU Censorship: We break down the latest European Union laws targeting independent media and the reposting of "banned" content.
Follow the Radical Thinker: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvJpN0vxULI9FjcCpOK4VPw

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #75 | JD Vance’s Diplomacy Fail: The "Persian Tactic" Confusion | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of "Cold War 2.0," historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar joins Kevork Almassian to deconstruct the shifting landscape of US-Iran relations. We break down the "Forever Talks" strategy, the JD Vance "Persian Tactic" controversy, and the explosive new developments in the Nordstream investigation that Germany is seemingly desperate to ignore.
Topics covered:
-The "Persian Tactic" Myth: Why JD Vance’s confusion over technical vs. peace talks exposes a lack of diplomatic experience. -Trump’s Iran Dilemma: Is the US "buying time" with the 60-day MOU, or has Tehran already secured a de facto victory? -The Law of Zionism: Analyzing the racist assumptions behind the US-Israeli targeted assassination strategy. -Germany’s "National Masochism": Why Berlin is reacting with hostility to its own investigation into the Ukrainian Nordstream commandos. -The Energy Trap: How German elites are prioritizing ideology over the vital infrastructure required for industrial survival.
Join us live and share your questions in the chat.

entry.title: Don't stop speaking up for Gaza, with Asem Alnabih
entry.summary:
It has now been 1,000 days since 7 October 2023 – 1,000 days of Israel’s genocide in Gaza.
It is clear: This was a genocide from the start.
Immediately, Israeli leaders declared their intentions openly. On 9 October 2023, then-defense minister – and indicted war criminal – Yoav Gallant announced what he called a “complete siege” on Gaza: “There will be no electricity, no food, no fuel, everything is closed. We are fighting human animals and we act accordingly.”
In January 2024, the International Court of Justice cited that and other statements as evidence of a plausible genocide and ordered Israel to halt all genocidal acts – a ruling it has ignored with impunity.
To reflect on this terrible milestone, we’re joined by Asem Alnabih in Gaza. Asem is an engineer, PhD researcher and was the spokesperson for Gaza Municipality. He is now a correspondent for Al Araby TV.
Asem has been in Gaza since the start of the genocide. He has written powerfully for The Electronic Intifada about survival, loss, family, starvation, memory and the dreams, hopes and futures Israel has tried to steal from the people of Gaza.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1000 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Asa Winstanley were joined writer, report and engineer Asem Alnabih in Gaza. You can watch the full show here: https://youtube.com/live/WQ0Hyp8r-wY
Theme Music by Greg Wilson
Your gift supports The Electronic Intifada's independent journalism on Palestine: • Donate by credit card, ApplePay, PayPal, Venmo or US bank account via Kindful: https://bit.ly/EIKindful
Gifts are welcome from anywhere and are tax-deductible for US taxpayers as allowed by law.

entry.title: This scandal says everything about the West's hypocrisy!
entry.summary:
The US-based right-wing Chinese billionaire Miles Guo (Guo Wengui), who worked closely with Donald Trump advisor Steve Bannon, was sentenced to 30 years in prison for committing $1 billion of fraud. Ben Norton explains how Western governments and media outlets support "dissidents" from countries they don't like, who are often corrupt grifters and con artists; and how fake "experts" make money by spreading absurd disinformation about China, Iran, Cuba, Venezuela, North Korea, etc.
Topics 0:00 Billionaire fraudster Miles Guo 1:03 Links to Trump, Steven Bannon 4:34 US DOJ prosecution 6:31 China's charges 8:06 US media whitewashing 12:25 Corrupt West-backed "dissidents" 15:23 US support for dictators 17:12 Pro-West Chinese "dissidents" 19:40 Soviet "dissidents" 21:07 North Korean defector Yeonmi Park 24:18 Yeonmi Park becomes a meme 25:36 (CLIP) Yeonmi Park on Joe Rogan 26:12 Absurd propaganda 26:53 Park jumps on the Trump train 27:47 Western media has no standards 28:56 Fake China "expert" Gordon G Chang 34:00 Ai Weiwei change of heart 39:16 Western imperialist hypocrisy 41:17 Outro

entry.title: Israel vs Turkey: The Armenian Genocide Proxy War
entry.summary:
Is Israel’s recognition of the Armenian Genocide a moral breakthrough or a calculated move in a larger proxy war? In this deep-dive, we peel back the layers of transactional politics—from F-35 deals in Washington to billions in arms sales to Azerbaijan.
As a descendant of genocide survivors, I analyze why the Netanyahu government is making this move now, the hidden role of the "smart villages" on the Iranian border, and the dangerous game being played with Syrian national identity. We also look at the sharp reactions from Turkey, the Armenian government, and why some see this as a "trolling" of Trump and NATO.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join

entry.title: Israel & Lebanon Shock Deal: Will Hezbollah Survive? | Marwa Osman
entry.summary:
In this high-stakes episode of Syriana Analysis, Marwa Osman joins us to dissect the recent "historic deal" signed between the Lebanese government and Israel in Washington. We dive deep into the geopolitical implications of this agreement and what it truly means for the future of Lebanon and the resistance.
Is this the beginning of a new era in the Middle East, or a calculated move to dismantle Hezbollah’s influence? We analyze the secret layers of the pact, Washington’s role as the mediator, and the potential shifts in regional power dynamics.
In this stream, we discuss:
- The core terms of the Lebanon-Israel deal. - Hezbollah's reaction and their survival strategy moving forward. - How this agreement impacts the "Resistance Axis." - The broader consequences for regional stability and sovereignty.

entry.title: The REAL reason US Big Tech corporations fear China
entry.summary:
US Big Tech corporations are paying influencers to spread anti-China, pro-AI disinformation on social media. Ben Norton explains why Silicon Valley fears Chinese competition and seeks to monopolize artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies, to impose the global dominance of the USA in an imperialist unipolar order.

entry.title: Socialist Humanism: The Struggle for LGBTQ+ Equality in Vietnam
entry.summary:
Vietnam has a vibrant and visible LGBTQ+ community which has made great progress in advancing equality in our socialist society. However, there are still many challenges to be overcome before Vietnam reaches its full potential in fulfilling the mission of socialist humanism. In this video, I share a speech I gave to a meeting of the Communist Party of Norway covering the topic of "Queer Marxism."
NOTE: The full event from the Communist Party of Norway has not yet been posted to YouTube, however, I will post it in the pinned comment below as soon as it becomes available.

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #74 | America’s Great Implosion: The Israel Factor | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian is joined by historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to analyze the profound shifts in the U.S.-Israel relationship and what Amar describes as America’s "Great Political Implosion." We dive into the Reuters report on Benjamin Netanyahu’s legacy, the rise of JD Vance and the "restrainer" narrative, and how shifting U.S. public opinion is redrawing the political map.
We also examine the latest UN Commission report on the targeting of children in Gaza and the broader geopolitical implications of a rising multipolar world involving China and the UK.
Kevork Birthday Special Offer: https://www.tarikcyrilamar.com/subscribe?coupon=5646299e
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy

entry.title: How FPV drones shaped Lebanon battle, with Jon Elmer
entry.summary:
Jon Elmer, contributing editor, reports on how Hizballah’s armed FPV drones and anti-tank missiles shaped the battle in South Lebanon before the US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding paused the fight earlier this week; and a breakdown of the Hizballah ambush that destroyed a Merkava tank, killing the commander of the Israeli military’s 52nd Armored Battalion during the battle at Ali al-Taher ridge. https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLHEvpbppx_4rb9lCZ9fRyQiTc5A7eS9FA

entry.title: Is Trump’s Iran Failure the Secret to Saving America?
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive analysis, we explore the aftermath of Donald Trump’s military tensions with Iran. While many see a tactical debacle, a closer look at the geopolitical shift suggests this failure may have inadvertently "saved" America by exposing the limits of military force and shifting the focus back to diplomacy and domestic stability.
Has the failure of "Maximum Pressure" actually prevented a larger regional war? We break down the long-term consequences for U.S. foreign policy, the shift in Middle Eastern power dynamics, and the "Israeli factor" behind the strategy.
Key Discussion Points:
0:00 – Intro: The "Debacle" Explained 6:20 – Strategic Costs: Why the War on Iran Failed 12:00 – The China & Russia Pivot: Depleting U.S. Resources 20:15 – The Israeli Factor: Regime Change vs. Regional Reality 26:40 – The End of Pan-Arabism & Rise of the Three Regional Projects 37:10 – Is This the "Secret Gift" for the American Public? 48:00 – The New Regional Order: Who Emerges Victorious? 55:50 – Closing: Is Diplomacy the Only Way Forward?
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Geopolitics #Iran #Trump #MiddleEastAnalysis #SyrianaAnalysis #ForeignPolicy #USNews

entry.title: Are the US and Israel really fighting? Or is something bigger happening?
entry.summary:
The USA and Iran are holding negotiations to end the war, but Tehran condemned Washington for violating the agreement, because Israel keeps attacking Lebanon. US Vice President JD Vance appeared to criticize Israel, but Ben Norton explains the deep hypocrisy of the United States, which is still giving Netanyahu billions of dollars of weapons and military aid.
Topics 0:00 Talks between USA & Iran 0:35 Iran says US violates agreement 1:50 Will Israel sabotage Iran deal? 2:58 Israel needs US military support 4:12 JD Vance: US is Israel's only ally 5:04 (CLIP) JD Vance: US military aid 5:47 (CLIP) JD Vance on Israel 7:57 Put up or shut up 8:40 Billions in US aid to Israel 9:42 Military-industrial complex 11:03 Unsinkable aircraft carrier 11:48 Tails don't wag dogs 12:36 Trump warns of oil shortages 13:25 (CLIP) Trump: 4 weeks of reserves 14:07 Trump threatens Iran again 14:33 (CLIP) Fox News on Trump threats 15:14 Iran has more leverage 16:08 Israel's goals in Lebanon 17:28 Israel takes over Gaza 18:40 US sends more weapons to Israel 19:14 Hypocrisy 19:45 Outro

entry.title: The War That Changed Me Forever | Kevork Almassian
entry.summary:
In this raw and personal 39th birthday special, I’m stepping away from the geopolitical maps of Syriana Analysis to share the real story of my journey—from growing up in Aleppo to seeking asylum in Germany. This is a reflection on the war that changed me forever and the personal battles I’ve fought behind the scenes.
For the first time, I discuss the weight of the smear campaigns and demonization I faced, the mental health breakdown that led me to the hospital in 2020, and the moment I realized that no one was coming to save me. I share how committing to fitness and "the gym" literally saved my life, helping me build the resilience needed to keep this channel independent and focused on the truth.
This video is for anyone facing their own demons, for the younger generation building their path, and for this incredible community that has supported me since 2017.
In this reflection, I cover:
Life in Aleppo: Growing up off-grid and the simplicity of nature. The Reality of War: How 14 years of conflict destroyed a nation and its intellectual class. Refugee Camps in Germany: The truth about my experience and the backlash that followed. The Turning Point: My 2020 hospital stay and how I reclaimed my physical and mental health. Building a Career, Not a Job: Why I left my easy job in Berlin to focus on Syrian Analysis. The Future of the Channel: New shows with Tarik Cyril Amar and our path forward. Thank you for 39 years of life lessons and for being the reason Syriana Analysis exists today.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
#SyrianaAnalysis #KevorkAlmassian #BirthdaySpecial #AleppoToBerlin #MentalHealth #Resilience #Geopolitics #IndependentMedia

entry.title: Why I destroyed Israeli drones, with Zoë Rogers
entry.summary:
Asa Winstanley, associate editor, speaks with Palestine Action activist Zoë Rogers, who was acquitted of criminal damage after spending 18 months in custody for breaking into an Elbit Systems factory in Bristol and helping destroy drones that could have been used against Palestinians.
Rogers, 22, was one of six activists who participated in the direct action in August 2024.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 986 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley were joined by former Palestine Action prisoner Zoë Rogers. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKhtKKgXNBw

entry.title: Colombians slam Trump's interference in presidential election
entry.summary:
US interference directed by the Trump administration has already influenced elections in Ecuador, Argentina, and Honduras, swinging votes to right-wing militarists determined to unravel what's left of their countries' social sectors while authorizing harsh repression against their opponents.
Now, as Colombia’s presidential runoff approaches, The Grayzone visits Huila, Colombia, near the border with Ecuador, to hear what voters think about Trump's backing of ultra-right candidate Abeldardo De La Espriella and his threats against the country if it elects leftist Ivan Cepeda.
Produced by Oscar Leon, shot by Bladimir Sanchez Espitia
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #73 | Deep State Exposed: Gabbard’s Bombshell & Trump’s Iran Gamble | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, host Kevork Almassian is joined by Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to analyze the seismic political shifts occurring during the transition of power. We dive deep into Tulsi Gabbard’s final actions as Director of National Intelligence, where she released never-before-seen documents exposing Dr. Fauci’s alleged role in suppressing the lab leak theory and his ties to the intelligence community.
We also tackle the "Trump Iran Peace Memo" revealed by The Economist. Is this a strategic masterstroke or a massive gamble that ignores decades of geopolitical reality? From the future of the MAGA movement to the rising alliance between Netanyahu and Modi, we break down the hidden forces shaping the new global order.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy

entry.content:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, host Kevork Almassian is joined by Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to analyze the seismic political shifts occurring during the transition of power. We dive deep into Tulsi Gabbard’s final actions as Director of National Intelligence, where she released never-before-seen documents exposing Dr. Fauci’s alleged role in suppressing the lab leak theory and his ties to the intelligence community.

We also tackle the "Trump Iran Peace Memo" revealed by The Economist. Is this a strategic masterstroke or a massive gamble that ignores decades of geopolitical reality? From the future of the MAGA movement to the rising alliance between Netanyahu and Modi, we break down the hidden forces shaping the new global order.

entry.title: Iran won the war, but will Israel wreck the deal to end it? with Ali Abunimah
entry.summary:
The United States and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to end the war of aggression launched by Washington and Tel Aviv on 28 February.
President Donald Trump made huge concessions to get Iran to sign, as the world was about to head over an economic cliff. There is no doubt that Iran won the war.
Ali Abunimah, executive director, reports on what is in the agreement. And although the text confirms Iran’s enormous leverage, it won’t necessarily be smooth sailing, as many difficult issues remain, and Israel, which continues to attack and occupy Lebanon and Gaza, will be determined to unravel the agreement.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 986 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley were joined by former Palestine Action prisoner Zoë Rogers. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKhtKKgXNBw

entry.title: US-Israel Split: New Middle East Map | Ehsan Safarnejad & Radical Thinker
entry.summary:
Is the decades-long US-Israel alliance in the Middle East reaching a breaking point?
In this deep-dive analysis, Ehsan Safarnejad and Radical Thinker join Syriana Analysis to break down the seismic shifts in the Middle East. As geopolitical dynamics shift, we explore whether the U.S. is pivoting its strategy in Lebanon and Syria—and what this means for Netanyahu’s government.
In this video, we discuss:
- The reported "split" between Washington and Tel Aviv. - The "New Middle East Map" and the role of Syria and Lebanon. - How regional players are reacting to the changing U.S. stance. - Strategic implications for the resistance axis and regional stability.
Follow Ehsan Safarnejad on X: https://x.com/Safarnejad_IR/ and check out his channel on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn-_UlidwJz0jTP1opyZbmw
Follow the Radical Thinker: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvJpN0vxULI9FjcCpOK4VPw
📌 Support the Channel: 🅿️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ⚡Crypto: https://wallet.rumble.com/tip/u/syrianaanalysis ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
00:00 - Introduction and welcome 01:03 - Reading the US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding 06:25 - Analysis of the war's outcome and the MOU 10:18 - Issues with sanction relief and legality 13:16 - The role of the US Congress and future threats 16:34 - Addressing criticisms from Professor David Miller 19:25 - The responsibility of Iranian leadership 21:18 - Shifts in US policy on uranium enrichment 22:56 - Potential for asymmetric warfare and future surprises 27:56 - Internal Iranian politics and diplomatic strategies 34:52 - Casualties and internal threats in Iran 41:51 - Israel's potential independent actions and political fallout 46:50 - Israel's influence and the global impact of the conflict 50:00 - Violations of the ceasefire and military capabilities 55:26 - Trump's suggestions on Syria and Hezbollah 56:12 - Deep dive into Israeli and Iranian strategies 01:02:40 - Syria's strategic importance and foreign involvement 01:10:56 - Jolani's role and the threat of sectarian war 01:13:02 - Why Hamas and Gaza were not in the deal 01:16:26 - Response to criticisms regarding Palestine support 01:29:14 - Final thoughts on Hamas, Gaza, and regional leverage 01:31:31 - World Cup boycott and treatment of the Iranian team 01:35:24 - Closing remarks and channel promotions
📢 Disclaimer: Guest opinions featured in this video do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #72 | Humiliated by Trump? Netanyahu May Set the Middle East Ablaze | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
Is the new US-Iran preliminary agreement a masterstroke of diplomacy or a strategic trap? Join Syriana Analysis and historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar for a "Cold War 2.0" deep dive into the shifting balance of power in the Middle East following the brokered ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.
In this episode, we break down the key elements of the Geneva memorandum, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the lifting of the US naval blockade, and the unfreezing of $24 billion in Iranian assets. We also examine the internal fallout in Israel, where Netanyahu faces mounting criticism for what many call a historic strategic failure.
In this video, we discuss:
- Why the US and Israel failed to achieve their primary war aims. - The "Western Preacher" mindset vs. the reality of regional chess players. - Whether Netanyahu will escalate in Gaza or Lebanon to stay in power. - The role of nuclear deterrence and unconventional warfare. - Turkey’s aggressive rhetoric vs. its actual policy toward Israel.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:

entry.title: 'Our only representative is the Resistance': Lebanese rally against negotiations w/ Israel
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Wyatt Reed reports from the Dahiya district of Beirut, Lebanon, where hundreds of supporters of Hezbollah rallied against the unconstitutional negotiations between the Lebanese government and Israel aimed at compelling Resistance factions to disarm.
The negotiations come as Israel bombards Lebanon's south, killing scores of civilians each week and destroying countless homes, while Iran conditions a deal with the US on a ceasefire in the country.

entry.title: Democracy is dead: Elon Musk becomes world's first TRILLIONAIRE with SpaceX IPO
entry.summary:

entry.title: I Lived in Lebanon: Here's Why They'll Never Disarm
entry.summary:
In this spontaneous video, I share my deeply personal journey through Lebanon—a country that defined my career and shaped my understanding of Middle Eastern geopolitics.
From my early days as an exchange student in Paris to finding my first professional footing in Beirut during the start of the Syrian war, I discuss the mentors and experiences that made me the journalist I am today. I also delve into the complex, often misunderstood reality of Lebanon's internal divisions, the lack of a unified national identity, and the heavy influence of foreign powers.
In this video, we explore:
- My Journey: How I moved from Aleppo to Beirut and landed my first job in media. - The Beirut Era: Working with Prof. Jamal Wakim and getting published in the legendary As-Safir newspaper. - The "Two Lebanons": The contrast between Beirut’s vibrant nightlife and the intense struggles of the south. - The Geopolitical Core: Why the question of disarmament is more than just political—it's a matter of survival for many in the region. - Identity & Resistance: An analysis of why Lebanon remains fragmented and the role of regional actors like Iran, Syria, and Israel. - Lebanon is a small country with a massive impact on the world stage. Whether you are interested in personal storytelling or high-level political analysis, this video aims to provide a perspective you won't find in mainstream media.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
00:00 - Introduction & A Rainy Day in Berlin 01:23 - Early Years: Studying in Paris & Moving to Lebanon 03:00 - Experience at the University of Balamand 05:25 - Starting a Career in Beirut Media 06:31 - Learning Geopolitics from Dr. Jamal Wakim 08:04 - Publishing My First Article in As-Safir Newspaper 11:53 - Why I Chose My Political Path in Syria 13:24 - The Unique Culture and Nightlife of Beirut 15:55 - The Fragmented National Identity of Lebanon 17:51 - Historical Roots: The Creation of Greater Lebanon 19:14 - Sectarian Democracy and Demographic Shifts 20:54 - Foreign Influence and Political Money in Lebanon 22:23 - Why the South Sees Disarmament as "Suicide" 24:44 - Facing Radical Threats: ISIS, Al-Qaeda, and the Jolani Side 27:42 - Iran’s Role and the New Rules of Engagement 29:06 - Critique of the Current Lebanese Government 31:35 - The Reality of US-Israeli Relations in the Region 33:57 - Lebanon's Geography and Its Place in the World 36:31 - The Role of Hezbollah in the Current Conflict 39:48 - Final Thoughts and Channel Update
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Lebanon #Geopolitics #MiddleEast #SyrianaAnalysis #Beirut

entry.title: Does Iran Have the Bomb? The New Rules Reshaping the Middle East | Mohammad Marandi
entry.summary:
As the conflict between Iran and Israel enters a dangerous new phase, the question of nuclear deterrence has moved from the shadows to the forefront of global geopolitics. In this LIVE broadcast, Professor Mohammad Marandi joins us to analyze whether the "rules of engagement" have permanently shifted and what this means for the future of the region.
In this stream, we explore:
- Nuclear Ambiguity: Analyzing the recent claims of a potential Iranian nuclear test and the reality of Iran's breakout capacity. - The New Rules of War: How Iran’s conventional strikes have challenged Israeli and US air superiority. - US-Israel Strategic Dilemma: Can Washington actually restrain Tel Aviv, or is a regional escalation now inevitable? - Resistance Strategy: The state of the "Unity of Fields" and the strategic depth of the IRGC in the current crisis.
Mohammad Marandi is a Professor of English Literature and Orientalism at the University of Tehran and a prominent political analyst on West Asian affairs.
📌 Support the Channel: 🅿️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ⚡Crypto: https://wallet.rumble.com/tip/u/syrianaanalysis ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
📢 Disclaimer: Guest opinions featured in this video do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.

entry.title: Iran vs Israel: Have the Rules of War Permanently Changed? | Elijah Magnier
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive geopolitical analysis, Elijah Magnier joined Syriana Analysis to break down whether Iran has established new rules of engagement against Israel and the shifting balance of power in the Middle East following recent missile strikes.
Topics covered include:
- Iran's new military doctrine and the "Rules of War." - The Trump & Netanyahu dynamic: Are they on the same page? - Syria's role under Jolani: Will they intervene in Lebanon? - Hezbollah's strategic role as a "hybrid actor" vs. an army. - The truth about IRGC involvement in southern Lebanon.
Elijah J. Magnier is a veteran war correspondent and a senior political risk analyst with over 38 years of experience covering West Asia.
Follow Elijah J. Magnier: https://x.com/ejmalrai Read Elijah J. Magnier's Work: https://ejmagnier.com/
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:

entry.title: Decline & Fall Live
Why countries are dropping the dollar - and buying gold
entry.summary:
Gold has officially overtaken Treasury securities (US government debt) as the top asset held by global central banks, according to a report by the European Central Bank (ECB). Ben Norton explains how dedollarization is continuing, gradually and steadily.

entry.title: Never stop talking about Gaza, with Ahmed Al-Najjar
entry.summary:
Israel killed 119 Palestinians in Gaza during May, the highest monthly toll since the start of the year.
Amid escalating massacres, Israel last Saturday murdered Dr. Jamal Abu Aoun, the head of the anaesthesia department at Al-Yafa Medical Hospital in central Gaza’s Deir al-Balah.
We speak to Ahmed Al-Najjar, a Palestinian journalist and academic based in Khan Younis, in the southern Gaza Strip.
We talk about Israel’s attacks, its expanding control of territory in Gaza, the role of collaborator gangs, as well as how people in Gaza are trying to cope and what they are hoping the future will bring.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 972 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley were joined by journalist Ahmed Al-Najjar. You can watch the full show here: https://youtube.com/live/WbWcsWEjO48

entry.title: Cold War 2.0 #71 | Rothschild & Kushner: Inside The $5 Billion Albania Takeover | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of "Cold War 2.0," host Kevork Almassian is joined by historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to unpack the multi-billion-dollar investment plans by Jared Kushner in Albania and their connection to Rothschild and broader Middle East strategy.
What we cover in this stream:
- Is Albania a "Resort" or a private island? We dive into the $5 billion project on Sazan Island and the Zvernec Peninsula. Is this simply luxury tourism, or a new secluded hub for global elites with no accountability?
- The Role of the Rothschilds & Qatar: Exploring the reported involvement of Nat Rothschild and Qatari investors in these massive infrastructure projects.
- The "Integration" of U.S. & Israeli Defense: We analyze the implications of Section 224 of the NDAA, which aims to fuse Israeli power into the American defense apparatus in a way that may be irreversible.
- Germany’s Diplomatic Defeat: Why did the UN General Assembly vote against Germany for a non-permanent Security Council seat? We discuss the fallout of Germany’s recent foreign policy decisions and their impact on international law.
- Iran & The Nuclear Question: Addressing recent rumors and the propaganda war surrounding Iran’s potential nuclear capabilities.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy

entry.title: “We will never leave”: Defying Israeli ethnic cleansing in South Lebanon
entry.summary:
In his latest on the ground report for The Grayzone, Wyatt Reed travelled to the ancient city of Tyre to document the aftermath of a brutal wave of Israeli strikes on residential buildings and local businesses.
Despite Israel’s claims that their attacks target Hezbollah, he found no evidence of militant activity. Instead, he only encountered civilians attempting to rebuild their lives — and, in many cases, refusing to obey a forced evacuation notice imposed by Israeli forces seeking to ethnically cleanse southern Lebanon.

entry.title: Before the AI bubble pops, billionaires want to take your retirement funds
entry.summary:
The AI bubble has caused the most concentrated and overvalued stock market in US history. The billionaire CEOs of Big Tech corporations fear it may pop soon, so they changed the rules on Wall Street so that, mere days after their IPOs, SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic will be given "fast track" inclusion in major index funds, despite the fact that they are losing tons of money.
These corporate oligarchs want to use average retail investors as exit liquidity, looting pensions, 401(k)s, and other retirement funds, all so Elon Musk can become the world's first trillionaire. Ben Norton explains the scandal.

entry.title: Did Iran Actually Test a Nuclear Device? We Checked the Claims
entry.summary:
Is the buzz about Iran’s "secret nuclear test" grounded in reality, or is it just more geopolitical hype? In this deep dive, Kevork Almassian is joined by Ehsan Safarnejad and Radical Thinker to dissect the sensationalist claims currently circulating in the media. We move past the headlines to look at the hard data, strategic motivations, and the reality of Iran’s nuclear threshold.

entry.title: Trump EXPLODES at Netanyahu as Israel Escalates in Lebanon | Marwa Osman
entry.summary:
Trump reportedly exploded at Netanyahu as Israel expands its military operations in Lebanon and risks dragging the region into a wider war. At the same time, Iran has suspended indirect talks with Washington, Hezbollah continues its resistance campaign, and Yemen warns it could enter the conflict. Syriana Analysis and Marwa Osman break down the growing rift between Trump and Netanyahu, the future of Lebanon, Iran's strategy, and where the Middle East is headed next.
Follow Marwa: https://x.com/Marwa__Osman
⚠️ Fast-moving situation: we will separate confirmed reporting from unverified claims and viral posts.

entry.title: Hezbollah leader to The Grayzone: Israel-First Trump 'turned America into a joke'
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Wyatt Reed interviews Wafiq Safa, a senior Hezbollah official and former head of Hezbollah's Liaison and Coordination Unit.
In a candid exchange about Hezbollah's conflict with Israel in Southern Lebanon, Safa provides new details on resistance operations, its intelligence capacity, and how he believes it has forced Israel into a costly stalemate that exposes the limits of Israeli military power.
Safa argues that any US-backed attempt to disarm Hezbollah would be illegitimate and could trigger civil war, because Hezbollah sees itself as part of a broader regional front that includes Iran and other anti-Israel forces.
Regarding Lebanon’s future, he argues that Israel’s talk of permanent settlement in the south is realistic only if resistance collapses, and he insists that the movement still stands by its decision to open the front after October 7 because it views the war as a defense of Lebanon’s sovereignty and a response to Israel’s wider regional project.
Safa also offers his assessment of Israeli influence on the US government, and whether Israeli is advancing US national security interests, or undermining them.

entry.title: US defeat in Iran War will change the world - Now even American hawks admit it!
entry.summary:

entry.title: This is the REAL reason for Trump's visit to China
entry.summary:
Donald Trump took a trip to China to meet President Xi Jinping. He was joined by the CEOs of a dozen top US corporations, including billionaires Elon Musk, Jensen Huang of Nvidia, Tim Cook of Apple, Larry Fink of BlackRock, and Stephen Schwarzman of Blackstone.
Ben Norton explains how Beijing won the trade war that was started by Washington, how China has significant leverage (in manufacturing, supply chains, critical minerals, and rare earths), and how the US economy is in a very bad state.

entry.title: Media admits: Iran destroyed US military bases, USA running out of missiles, war costs $1 TRILLION
entry.summary:
Donald Trump claims Iran has been "militarily defeated", but many reports have come out showing this to be a lie. Tehran destroyed the majority of US military bases in West Asia (aka the Middle East), and Iran still maintains most of its missile capabilities.
Meanwhile, experts estimate the USA's war against Iran will cost $1 trillion.
Ben Norton reviews the evidence, showing how Washington is clearly losing this war that it started.

entry.title: Historian Dismantle the “Trump 4D Chess” Theory on Iran
entry.summary:
Is Trump really playing 4D chess in the Iran war or is that just a way of rationalizing strategic failure? In this segment, Tarik Cyril Amar dismantles the theory that Washington is secretly executing a flawless long-term plan in West Asia through the UAE, energy corridors, and controlled destabilization. He argues that this theory ignores the obvious: Trump is irrational, U.S. policy is deeply chaotic, Israel exerts enormous influence, and the war has damaged American credibility rather than strengthened it.

entry.title: Why Germany Wants to Build Europe’s Strongest Army By 2039
entry.summary:
Why does Germany want to build Europe’s strongest army by 2039? In this segment, Tarik Cyril Amar breaks down what Berlin’s rearmament drive is really about, why it goes far beyond simple “defense,” and how the war in Iran has accelerated German ambitions in Europe. He argues that Germany is not merely preparing to protect its own territory but positioning itself for a much larger geopolitical role tied to Ukraine, the Baltics, and continued U.S. power projection.

entry.title: Why the US pressured the UAE to leave OPEC: Big Oil corporations benefit
entry.summary:
OPEC, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, is in crisis after the United Arab Emirates (UAE), its third-largest producer, left the group. Donald Trump openly praised the decision, saying it is good for the United States. Ben Norton explains why the US government opposes OPEC, which is rooted in the Third World nationalist movements of the 1960s, which sought more economic resources for the Global South. The USA wants its Big Oil corporations to dominate the industry.

entry.title: How the US uses oil as a geopolitical weapon
entry.summary:
Oil has been at the center of the war on Iran. Ben Norton explains how the United States became the biggest oil producer on Earth, and how the US empire uses petroleum as a geopolitical and economic weapon, pushing for monopolistic Big Oil corporations to dominate the global market. Ben also discusses why Trump pressured the UAE to withdraw from OPEC, and he debunks the myth that the USA is "energy independent".
Check out our related video on the biggest oil crisis in history: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bUdyPJM_rOw

entry.title: US economy is based on Ponzi scheme that could collapse, warns economist Michael Hudson
entry.summary:
There are growing signs that the United States may be on the verge of another major financial crisis, one that could start in the $3 trillion private credit market, which is already seeing significant turmoil, before spreading to other sectors.
Geopolitical Economy Report editor Ben Norton interviewed economist Michael Hudson to discuss the serious problems on Wall Street. Hudson warned that the US economy is built on a Ponzi scheme that depends on continuing to pour money into a bloated, bubbly financial system based on unsustainable speculation, not industrial production.
Check out our related short video about the private credit crisis on Wall Street: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S1cx9Zk6WZk
Topics 0:00 Introduction 1:37 Highlights of Michael Hudson 3:29 Interview starts 4:18 2008 financial crisis 5:04 Zero interest-rate policy (ZIRP) 7:31 Private equity 9:17 Lack of investment 10:34 Wealth inequality 12:08 Debt defaults 14:10 Depression fears 16:12 US govt supports Wall Street 19:07 Ponzi schemes 23:51 US economic problems 27:35 Iran war impact 30:16 Financialization of economy 33:46 Outro
|| Geopolitical Economy Report ||

entry.title: Vietnam's Textbook Privatization Disaster
entry.summary:
School textbooks are a vital social institution for a socialist nation. Since the 1950s, Vietnam has had several textbook reforms, with varying degrees of success. Unfortunately, in 2013, Vietnam privatized the writing and publishing of school textbooks, leading to severe problems. In this video, we examine the catastrophic failure of textbook privatization in Vietnam.

entry.title: Why is Trump blockading Iran's blockade? This is the crazy, and dangerous, US strategy
entry.summary:
The peace talks held between the US and Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan failed. Despite a temporary, two-week ceasefire Donald Trump announced a naval blockade of the Iranian blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Ben Norton explains the USA's strategy, and how it is targeting China and Asian economies that import oil, natural gas, and other important commodities from the Persian Gulf.

entry.title: What just happened in China is huge
entry.summary:
The US government has pushed for years for a war with China, selling billions of dollars of weapons to separatists in Taiwan. However, those plans are falling apart, after the leader of the KMT, the Chinese Nationalist Party based in Taipei, took a historic trip to the mainland, promoting peace. Ben Norton explains the importance of Cheng Li-wun's meeting with Xi Jinping, and the complex political history of Taiwan.

entry.title: Tarik Cyril Amar: Norman Finkelstein Is Wrong on Israel's War With Iran
entry.summary:
Historian Tarik Cyril Amar responds to Dr. Norman Finkelstein’s analysis of Israel’s war with Iran and argues that Finkelstein gets several key points wrong. In this segment, Tarik says Finkelstein makes “colossal mistakes” by too quickly dismissing possible Israeli influence in major U.S. decisions, by treating Donald Trump as if he could not be fooled, and by downplaying how deeply Israeli intelligence and pro-Israel networks penetrate the American system. Tarik also argues that the Iraq war and the war on Iran cannot be separated so easily from the neoconservative-Israeli nexus, even if Washington ultimately bears primary responsibility. The core of Tarik’s criticism is respectful but sharp: Finkelstein is right to insist that the United States is to blame, but wrong in the way he argues the case.
Subscribe to Tarik's Substack: https://www.tarikcyrilamar.com/subscribe?coupon=169c702d

entry.title: The Iran war changes everything: The world will never be the same
entry.summary:
The US-Israeli war on Iran is transforming the geopolitical order, and could even unleash a global economic crisis. The conflict has caused the largest oil shock in history, disrupting markets and driving up fuel and food prices.
To better understand the implications for the world, Geopolitical Economy Report editor Ben Norton interviewed economist Michael Hudson, who discussed how Iran is challenging US dollar dominance and undermining Washington’s control over the global oil industry, which has been a key pillar of the USA's foreign policy.
Read the transcript here: https://www.geopoliticaleconomy.report/p/war-iran-change-economy-michael-hudson

entry.title: The secret plan behind the war on Iran
entry.summary:
The war on Iran is part of a larger attempt by the US empire to reverse the decline of Western hegemony and recolonize the Global South. After invading Venezuela, Donald Trump wants to "take" Cuba, Greenland, and Gaza. Meanwhile, Israel is annexing the West Bank and colonizing southern Lebanon. Ben Norton explains the imperialist plan.

entry.title: Big blow to US dollar: Iran says oil must be sold in Chinese yuan, as it targets US corporations
entry.summary:
In response to the US-Israeli war, Iran shut down the Strait of Hormuz, the most important oil chokepoint on Earth, causing energy prices to skyrocket. However, Tehran is allowing Chinese tankers through, and says other ships can pass if they agree to sell oil in China's currency, the renminbi (aka yuan).
Iran is also targeting offices of major American corporations, and wants to force them out of the Middle East (West Asia), while trying to expel US military bases.
Ben Norton explains how this war affects the petrodollar system, and the dominance of the dollar as the global reserve currency.
Topics 0:00 Iran war causes energy crisis 0:29 Oil price skyrockets 0:56 Fears of economic crisis 2:07 Strait of Hormuz 3:21 Iran permits Chinese oil tankers 4:17 Iran: sell oil in RMB, not USD 4:43 Dollar dominance & petrodollar 6:28 Asymmetric warfare 6:59 US military bases in Middle East 7:47 Iran strikes CIA stations 8:46 Iran hits US refueling planes 9:10 US empire underestimated Iran 10:32 Iran targets US corporations 12:03 Economic warfare 13:07 CLIP Scott Bessent on Iran 14:05 New leader Mojtaba Khamenei 15:21 Iran strategy spelled out 17:08 Tehran wants revenge 18:51 Yemen, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandab 22:16 US war on Yemen in 2025 23:23 Anti-colonial wars 24:16 Trump sends more US troops 25:21 Iran's Kharg Island 26:21 Global energy crisis 27:24 Trump wants military coalition 29:02 Iran allows Chinese ships 29:59 US dollar dominance 30:37 US current account deficit 31:20 Exorbitant privilege of dollar 32:19 Dedollarization 32:52 West seized Russia's reserves 33:49 Central banks buy gold 34:16 US Treasury securities 34:47 Oil sales in dollars 36:26 Trump threatened BRICS 37:16 Iran promotes dedollarization 37:42 China & Russia de-dollarize 38:33 India 39:34 UAE 40:18 Saudi Arabia 41:04 Iran & Indonesia join BRICS 42:14 Iran proposes BRICS currency 42:31 Divisions within BRICS 43:12 Struggle against imperialism 45:12 Outro

entry.title: Tarik Cyril Amar: Why Iran Can’t Compromise — and Why Trump Hesitates
entry.summary:
Trump Iran talks, “compromise,” Netanyahu pressure, Iran missile deterrent, and the risk of war—in this Syriana Analysis clip, historian Tarik Cyril Amar explains why concessions rarely end pressure, and why Trump may hesitate.
In this segment, Tarik breaks down:
● Why “compromise” can become a trap that invites more demands
● The role of messaging and pressure politics in Washington
● Why Iran’s deterrence posture (especially missiles) is treated as a red-line issue
● Why a major war would be unpredictable and costly—making hesitation rational even for hardliners
● How diplomacy, threats, and media narratives can be used together as leverage
This clip is an analysis and commentary based on publicly reported developments and historical patterns. The goal is clarity, not hype.

entry.title: The REAL Issue with the Epstein Files: Power & Influence | Kevork Almassian
entry.summary:
Epstein files, power and influence networks, elite protection, media narratives, and what’s actually known — in this video, Kevork Almassian breaks down the REAL issue behind the document drop: not gossip, but how power works.
This isn’t tabloid entertainment. It’s a case study in access, reputation management, and institutional protection — how certain circles gain leverage, shape narratives, and survive scandal. We separate what’s documented from what’s alleged, and focus on incentives: who benefits from specific framings, and why some stories dominate while others disappear.
In this video:

entry.title: EU Blacklist: Jacques Baud and Nathalie Yamb Targeted for ‘Hybrid Threats’ | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
EU Blacklist: Jacques Baud and Nathalie Yamb Targeted for ‘Hybrid Threats’ | Syriana Analysis
EU measures against so-called “Russian hybrid threats”, information manipulation and alleged propaganda – and what this means for free speech and civil liberties in Europe – are the focus of this video on Syriana Analysis. On 15 December 2025, the Council of the EU expanded its “hybrid threats” listing regime “in view of Russia’s destabilising activities,” adding new individuals and entities. Those listed face asset freezes and travel bans, with serious consequences for their banking, contracts, mobility and reputation.
Officially, this toolbox targets foreign information manipulation and interference (FIMI), cyber activity and other hybrid actions linked to the Russian state. But the Council’s own explanations now include terms such as “propaganda,” “conspiracy theories” and “information manipulation” when it justifies adding people to the list. In this monologue I ask a simple question:
👉 When the EU freezes a person’s assets and blocks their travel because of what they say, what safeguards exist for free expression and due process? I explain how these restrictive measures work, how they affect the lives of commentators and public figures, and why using executive asset freezes against people like Jacques Baud or Nathalie Yamb raises serious concerns about proportionality, transparency and legal standards.
🧠 In this video we cover: • The new EU “hybrid threats” framework and FIMI toolbox • How “propaganda”, “conspiracy theories” and “information manipulation” appear in EU justifications • The difference between prosecuting espionage or cyber attacks and punishing speech • Why asset freezes and travel bans are not a “debate” but an exercise of power • What safeguards and review mechanisms are needed if Europe wants to remain credible as a defender of democracy
This is not about agreeing with any particular commentator. It is about asking whether administrative blacklisting is the right tool to answer controversial opinions – and what happens to European democracy if criticism can be branded a “hybrid threat” by political decision.
🔔 Subscribe to Syriana Analysis for independent coverage of Europe, Russia, hybrid warfare and global geopolitics. 👍 If you found this useful, please like, comment and share – especially with friends in the EU who care about civil liberties.

entry.title: American Parents Need to Hear Why Their Sons Are in Syria | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
US troops in Syria and American soldiers deployed in the Middle East are at the heart of this video. A Syrian talks directly to the American public and asks a simple question: Why is the US military still in Syria after so many years of war? This video highlights the complex "international relations" and ongoing "conflict" in the region, offering a perspective often missing from mainstream "world news" and "us news" reports about the "Syrian civil war" and the broader "Middle East".
Many Americans have been told a straightforward story about why US forces went into Syria: • to protect the homeland, • to confront a dangerous group, • to promote stability and democracy.
This video offers a different perspective.
I explain how Syria became a central arena of US foreign policy, how large-scale programs supported armed groups on the ground, and how the conflict was shaped long before most Americans had even heard of places like al-Tanf or eastern Syria’s oil and wheat regions.
I then outline four main reasons why US troops were deployed and kept in Syria: • control over key energy fields, • presence at important transport corridors, • influence over agricultural areas that sustain millions of people, • and the ability to shape any future political settlement.
Today, with a completely new leadership structure in Damascus, US personnel are still on the ground, working alongside local partners and carrying out joint activities far from American soil.
The central question of this video is simple:
👉 If you believe the US military should primarily defend American territory and citizens, what exactly is the long-term mission of US troops in Syria today?
This is not about hostility toward the United States. It is about asking whether current policy in Syria truly serves the American nation and its families, or whether it mainly serves distant strategic and economic agendas.
